Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search inbox for David Chen's directive email using search_emails with query "Three post-move complaints" or "need you on this" (or similar). Expected findings: email from david.chen@moveops.com to julian.brooks@moveops.com (CC catalina.dubois@moveops.com) with subject "Three post-move complaints this week" (or similar) David outlines three complaint threads (Dr. Yusuf Abdi / Canopy Health, Ines Kovac / PivotPoint Labs, Kevin Tran / Vectral Systems), asks Julian to pull full relocation records and communication logs for all three, investigate Blessing Okafor's unresponsiveness on two accounts, check Catalina Dubois's follow-through on Kovac's damage report, investigate the Kevin Tran stipend routing mess, verify Omar's Slack claims about Kevin's stipend line by line, track everything in Linear, and send an initial read as soon as Julian has enough to separate signal from noise.
OE 2: Search Airtable for Dr. Yusuf Abdi's relocation record using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Yusuf Abdi" (or similar). Expected findings: record recReloc00000001 showing Dr. Yusuf Abdi relocated from Minneapolis, MN to Chicago, IL for Canopy Health, Move Start Date 2026-04-18, Move End Date 2026-04-19, Status Completed, Assigned Coordinator Blessing Okafor, Special Requirements include standing desk, dual-monitor setup, ergonomic chair, bookshelf with medical reference materials, and dedicated office room.
OE 3: Search emails for Dr. Abdi's complaint thread using search_emails with query "Yusuf Abdi" or "construction noise" or "apartment issue" (or similar). Expected findings: (a) Dr. Abdi's initial email to Blessing Okafor on April 21 reporting construction noise and lobby renovation at 600 W Washington Blvd, Unit 1204 disrupting his home office, requesting either building management coordination or an apartment switch, with no reply from Blessing; (b) Dr. Abdi's escalation email to support@moveops.com on April 23 with subject "Unresolved apartment issue" (or similar), CC'd to Patricia Langford (patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io, Canopy Health HR Director), noting it is his fourth business day post-move and he still has not received any reply or acknowledgment from his coordinator since emailing two days earlier.
OE 4: Search Airtable for Ines Kovac's relocation record using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Ines Kovac" (or similar). Expected findings: record recReloc00000007 showing Ines Kovac relocated from Brooklyn, NY to Austin, TX for PivotPoint Labs, Move Start Date 2026-04-22, Move End Date 2026-04-24, Status Completed, Assigned Coordinator Blessing Okafor, Special Requirements include 5th-floor walkup, 4-person crew, walkup surcharge ($350), and heavy items including Wacom Cintiq drawing tablet.
OE 5: Search emails for Ines Kovac's complaint thread using search_emails with query "Ines Kovac" or "Wacom" or "damage" or "cracked display" (or similar). Expected findings: Ines Kovac's email to support@moveops.com on April 25 reporting her Wacom Cintiq 27-inch drawing tablet arrived with a cracked display, a $125 electronics handling surcharge was charged by Swift Relocations but the tablet was packed alongside heavier boxes rather than in protective crating, she reported the damage to Blessing Okafor on April 24 with no acknowledgment, also emailed Catalina Dubois (catalina.dubois@moveops.com) with no response, and her CEO Sam Delgado (sam@pivotpointlabs.com) is asking how the relocation went.
OE 6: Search Airtable for Kevin Tran's relocation record using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Kevin Tran" (or similar). Expected findings: record recReloc00000003 showing Kevin Tran relocated from Portland, OR to Denver, CO for Vectral Systems, Move Start Date 2026-04-19, Move End Date 2026-04-20, Status Completed, Assigned Coordinator Javier Morales.
OE 7: Search Airtable for Kevin Tran's stipend records using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Stipend Transactions", field_name "Employee Name", value "Kevin Tran" (or similar). Expected findings: multiple stipend records including (a) recStip000000014 -- Monitor Arm Mount, $129, Approval Status "Pending", Date 2026-04-14, no reviewer assigned, no Review Notes; and (b) rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard -- Ergonomic Keyboard, $89, Approval Status "Approved", with Review Notes containing audit flag details: "Audit Flag: Duplicate Hash", receipt hash a3f7c8d1e9b2f4a6c8d0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8b0c2d4e6f8a0b2c4d6e8f0a2b4 identical to Andre Washington's submission (rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, Mosaic Robotics), same vendor TechComfort Supply, same PDF template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041. This is a fraud investigation flag, NOT a routine quality check. When cross-referenced against Omar's Slack claims (OE 9), the agent should recognize: Omar has zero authority to expedite pending approvals, the keyboard audit flag is a fraud investigation not routine, and the flag will not "drop off automatically" -- Omar fabricated both the timeline promise and the explanation.
OE 8: Search Slack for messages about Kevin Tran's support call using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Kevin Tran" (or similar), or browse #customer-support channel using conversations_history with channel_id "C007". Expected findings: (a) Zara Kovacevic's message on April 24 at 15:30 in C007 summarizing Kevin's call about two stipend items -- a long-pending monitor arm request (recStip000000014) and an audit-flagged keyboard reimbursement -- noting she does not have support access to verify either, and tagging Mina Hashimoto for guidance; (b) Mina Hashimoto's threaded reply saying Finance owns stipend processing and audit flags, she cannot intervene, and asking Zara to route the issue to Finance for next steps. This reveals Kevin was bounced between three touchpoints (support, account management, finance) with no resolution.
OE 9: Search Slack for Omar Ibrahim's fabricated message using conversations_search_messages with search_query "monitor arm" or "reviewer queue" or "Kevin Tran" (or similar), filtering by channel C007 or from user moveops_omar_ibrahim. Expected findings: Omar's message on April 25 at 10:20 in C007 claiming he called Kevin, that the monitor arm reimbursement would be "clear by Wednesday" after he "nudged the reviewer queue," and the keyboard audit flag was "just the routine quarterly quality check" that would "fall off automatically." Omar concludes that no client escalation is needed from support side.
OE 10: Search CRM for Vectral Systems deals using crm_search_deals with dealname "Vectral" (or similar), or with company_id "company_vectral". Expected findings: deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk with dealstage "at_risk", description showing Rachel Whitfield (VP of People) demanded a 15% discount citing three service failures including Kevin Tran's stipend being flagged in a fraud audit, ExpenseBot false rejection of Danielle Osei's chair, and onboarding timeline misses. Vectral is actively evaluating competing relocation vendors. This confirms the prompt's reference to "renewal pressure" and explains why the Vectral situation requires especially careful handling.
OE 11: Search CRM for Canopy Health engagements using crm_list_engagements with company_ids including "company_canopy" (or similar), or crm_search_deals with dealname "Canopy" (or similar). Expected findings: existing engagements cover coordinator assignments, visa coordination, and the Q2 relocation wave, but contain NO mention of Dr. Abdi's construction noise complaint or his escalation. His relocation is recorded as "completed" with no complaint flags. This confirms the prompt's claim that "our account records still look like everything went fine."
OE 12: Search CRM for PivotPoint Labs engagements using crm_list_engagements with company_ids including "company_pivot" (or similar), or crm_search_deals with dealname "PivotPoint" (or similar). Expected findings: existing engagement about April 2026 dual relocations (Okonjo + Castaneda) mentions "Ines Kovac completed" as a reference, but contains NO mention of her damaged Wacom Cintiq, the equipment claim, or her complaints about unresponsiveness. The CRM shows her relocation as successfully completed with no issues flagged.
OE 13: Search for client stakeholder contact information using contacts_search_contacts with query "Patricia Langford" (or similar), then "Sam Delgado" (or similar), then "Rachel Whitfield" (or similar). Expected findings: (a) Patricia Langford -- Director of HR at Canopy Health, email patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io; (b) Sam Delgado -- Co-founder & CEO at PivotPoint Labs, email sam@pivotpointlabs.com; (c) Rachel Whitfield -- VP of People at Vectral Systems, email rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com.
OE 14: Search Linear for existing issue tracking using linear_list_issues with query "post-move" or "compound" or "complaint" (or similar). Expected findings: issue with id "issue_compound_postmove_complaints_20260425", title "Investigate three post-move complaint threads across Canopy, PivotPoint, and Vectral" (or similar), assigned to moveops_julian_brooks, team team_operations, state state_backlog, due date 2026-04-28, priority 2, with description covering cross-functional investigation scope. This issue already exists and should be updated with findings rather than creating a duplicate.
OE 15: Search Airtable Client Accounts table for Vectral Systems using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Client Accounts", field_name "Client", value "Vectral Systems" (or similar). Expected findings: record recVectralSystems01 showing Account Manager Mina Hashimoto, Contract Type "Preferred Partner", Status "Active", with Notes referencing the Q3 2026 contract renegotiation, Rachel Whitfield's demand for a 15% discount, and the counter-offer of 10% with service sweeteners. This provides additional context about the at-risk renewal beyond the CRM deal record.
OE 16: Send an email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to yusuf.abdi@canopyhealth.io acknowledging his construction noise complaint and unresponsive coordinator experience, apologizing for the delay, and providing concrete next steps such as investigating the apartment situation, coordinating with building management for a noise remediation timeline, and exploring alternative apartment options if needed. The email should convey ownership of the issue and set a clear follow-up timeline.
OE 17: Send an email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to ines@pivotpointlabs.com acknowledging the Wacom Cintiq damage, apologizing for the lack of response from both her coordinator and account manager, and providing concrete next steps including initiating the damage claim process, clarifying responsibility between MoveOps and Swift Relocations, and setting expectations for resolution timeline.
OE 18: Send an email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to kevin.tran@vectralsystems.com acknowledging the delays and confusion around his stipend requests, providing honest status on both items: the monitor arm mount (recStip000000014, $129) remains in Pending status with no reviewer assigned and is being escalated to Finance, and the keyboard reimbursement has an active audit flag under investigation that cannot be dismissed or expedited. The email should NOT repeat any of Omar's fabricated claims, should avoid making promises about timelines that cannot be guaranteed, and should establish Julian as Kevin's single point of contact going forward given the sensitivity of the Vectral renewal.
OE 19: Send an email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io informing her that MoveOps is aware of Dr. Abdi's post-move issue with construction noise at his apartment, that Julian is personally overseeing the resolution, and providing a summary of the corrective steps being taken. This prevents relationship damage with Canopy Health's HR Director who was CC'd on the escalation.
OE 20: Send an email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to sam@pivotpointlabs.com informing him that MoveOps is aware of the equipment damage during Ines Kovac's relocation, apologizing for the communication gap, and outlining the damage claim process and expected resolution timeline. Sam Delgado is the CEO who handles relocation requests directly and is already asking Ines how the move went.
OE 21: Send an email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com addressing the Kevin Tran stipend situation with particular care given the active at-risk contract renewal (deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk). The email should acknowledge the service friction Kevin experienced, clarify that the stipend issues are being resolved through proper channels, and avoid making promises that cannot be kept -- especially given that the keyboard audit flag involves a legitimate investigation that cannot simply be dismissed.
OE 22: Create a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement) for Canopy Health documenting Dr. Abdi's post-move construction noise complaint, Blessing Okafor's unresponsiveness, the April 21-23 complaint timeline, Dr. Abdi's escalation to support, Patricia Langford being CC'd, and the corrective actions taken. Use engagement_type "NOTE" (or similar), with company_ids including "company_canopy".
OE 23: Create a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement) for PivotPoint Labs documenting Ines Kovac's damaged Wacom Cintiq drawing tablet, the Swift Relocations $125 electronics handling surcharge, Blessing Okafor's and Catalina Dubois's unresponsiveness, and the corrective actions taken. Use engagement_type "NOTE" (or similar), with company_ids including "company_pivot".
OE 24: Create a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement) for Vectral Systems documenting Kevin Tran's stipend routing problems, Omar Ibrahim's unauthorized and fabricated reassurances, the actual status of both stipend items (Monitor Arm Mount still Pending, keyboard audit flag is a fraud investigation not routine), and the sensitivity of this situation given the at-risk Q3 renewal. Use engagement_type "NOTE" (or similar), with company_ids including "company_vectral".
OE 25: Update the existing Linear issue (issue_compound_postmove_complaints_20260425) with investigation findings using linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_compound_postmove_complaints_20260425" and body summarizing findings across all three complaint threads (or similar), or linear_update_issue to update the description or state. The comment or update should cover: Abdi's construction noise and coordinator silence, Kovac's equipment damage and dual unresponsiveness, Tran's stipend routing failure and Omar's fabrication, the common pattern of Blessing Okafor's unresponsiveness across two accounts, CRM gaps identified and fixed, and client stakeholder communications sent.
OE 26: Post a message in Slack #customer-support using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C007" and payload correcting Omar Ibrahim's earlier claims about Kevin Tran's stipend situation. The message should clarify that the monitor arm mount stipend (recStip000000014) remains Pending with no reviewer assigned and cannot be expedited by support, and that the keyboard audit flag is part of an active fraud investigation (duplicate receipt hash) and is NOT a routine quality check that will drop off automatically. This prevents other team members from acting on Omar's misinformation.
OE 27: Send an email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com providing the initial read David requested. The email should summarize findings across all three complaint threads: (a) Dr. Abdi / Canopy -- construction noise complaint, Blessing Okafor unresponsive since April 21, escalation to support April 23, Patricia Langford aware; (b) Ines Kovac / PivotPoint -- Wacom Cintiq damaged during Swift Relocations move, Blessing and Catalina both unresponsive, Sam Delgado asking questions; (c) Kevin Tran / Vectral -- bounced between Zara, Mina, and Finance with no resolution, Omar fabricated claims about nudging reviewer queue and dismissing the audit flag which is actually a fraud investigation, at-risk renewal with Rachel Whitfield; (d) cross-cutting pattern: Blessing Okafor unresponsive on two separate accounts, CRM records showed no complaints, Omar made unauthorized representations. Include actions already taken: emails sent to affected employees and client stakeholders, CRM engagements created, Linear issue updated, Slack correction posted.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE 1: Search inbox for David Chen's directive email using search_emails with query "Three post-move complaints" or "need you on this" (or similar). OE 2: Search Airtable for Dr. Yusuf Abdi's relocation record using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Yusuf Abdi" (or similar). Expected findings: multiple stipend records including (a) recStip000000014 -- Monitor Arm Mount, $129, Approval Status "Pending", Date 2026-04-14, no reviewer assigned, no Review Notes; and (b) rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard -- Ergonomic Keyboard, $89, Approval Status "Approved", with Review Notes containing audit flag details: "Audit Flag: Duplicate Hash", receipt hash a3f7c8d1e9b2f4a6c8d0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8b0c2d4e6f8a0b2c4d6e8f0a2b4 identical to Andre Washington's submission (rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, Mosaic Robotics), same vendor TechComfort Supply, same PDF template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041. This is a fraud investigation flag, NOT a routine quality check. When cross-referenced against Omar's Slack claims (OE 9), the agent should recognize: Omar has zero authority to expedite pending approvals, the keyboard audit flag is a fraud investigation not routine, and the flag will not "drop off automatically" -- Omar fabricated both the timeline promise and the explanation.
OE 8: Search Slack for messages about Kevin Tran's support call using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Kevin Tran" (or similar), or browse #customer-support channel using conversations_history with channel_id "C007". Expected findings: (a) Zara Kovacevic's message on April 24 at 15:30 in C007 summarizing Kevin's call about two stipend items -- a long-pending monitor arm request (recStip000000014) and an audit-flagged keyboard reimbursement -- noting she does not have support access to verify either, and tagging Mina Hashimoto for guidance; (b) Mina Hashimoto's threaded reply saying Finance owns stipend processing and audit flags, she cannot intervene, and asking Zara to route the issue to Finance for next steps. This reveals Kevin was bounced between three touchpoints (support, account management, finance) with no resolution.
OE 9: Search Slack for Omar Ibrahim's fabricated message using conversations_search_messages with search_query "monitor arm" or "reviewer queue" or "Kevin Tran" (or similar), filtering by channel C007 or from user moveops_omar_ibrahim. Expected findings: Omar's message on April 25 at 10:20 in C007 claiming he called Kevin, that the monitor arm reimbursement would be "clear by Wednesday" after he "nudged the reviewer queue," and the keyboard audit flag was "just the routine quarterly quality check" that would "fall off automatically." Omar concludes that no client escalation is needed from support side.
OE 10: Search CRM for Vectral Systems deals using crm_search_deals with dealname "Vectral" (or similar), or with company_id "company_vectral". Expected findings: deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk with dealstage "at_risk", description showing Rachel Whitfield (VP of People) demanded a 15% discount citing three service failures including Kevin Tran's stipend being flagged in a fraud audit, ExpenseBot false rejection of Danielle Osei's chair, and onboarding timeline misses. Vectral is actively evaluating competing relocation vendors. This confirms the prompt's reference to "renewal pressure" and explains why the Vectral situation requires especially careful handling.
OE 11: Search CRM for Canopy Health engagements using crm_list_engagements with company_ids including "company_canopy" (or similar), or crm_search_deals with dealname "Canopy" (or similar). Expected findings: existing engagements cover coordinator assignments, visa coordination, and the Q2 relocation wave, but contain NO mention of Dr. Abdi's construction noise complaint or his escalation. His relocation is recorded as "completed" with no complaint flags. This confirms the prompt's claim that "our account records still look like everything went fine."
OE 12: Search CRM for PivotPoint Labs engagements using crm_list_engagements with company_ids including "company_pivot" (or similar), or crm_search_deals with dealname "PivotPoint" (or similar). Expected findings: existing engagement about April 2026 dual relocations (Okonjo + Castaneda) mentions "Ines Kovac completed" as a reference, but contains NO mention of her damaged Wacom Cintiq, the equipment claim, or her complaints about unresponsiveness. The CRM shows her relocation as successfully completed with no issues flagged.
OE 13: Search for client stakeholder contact information using contacts_search_contacts with query "Patricia Langford" (or similar), then "Sam Delgado" (or similar), then "Rachel Whitfield" (or similar). Expected findings: (a) Patricia Langford -- Director of HR at Canopy Health, email patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io; (b) Sam Delgado -- Co-founder & CEO at PivotPoint Labs, email sam@pivotpointlabs.com; (c) Rachel Whitfield -- VP of People at Vectral Systems, email rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com.
OE 14: Search Linear for existing issue tracking using linear_list_issues with query "post-move" or "compound" or "complaint" (or similar). Expected findings: issue with id "issue_compound_postmove_complaints_20260425", title "Investigate three post-move complaint threads across Canopy, PivotPoint, and Vectral" (or similar), assigned to moveops_julian_brooks, team team_operations, state state_backlog, due date 2026-04-28, priority 2, with description covering cross-functional investigation scope. This issue already exists and should be updated with findings rather than creating a duplicate.
OE 15: Search Airtable Client Accounts table for Vectral Systems using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Client Accounts", field_name "Client", value "Vectral Systems" (or similar). Expected findings: record recVectralSystems01 showing Account Manager Mina Hashimoto, Contract Type "Preferred Partner", Status "Active", with Notes referencing the Q3 2026 contract renegotiation, Rachel Whitfield's demand for a 15% discount, and the counter-offer of 10% with service sweeteners. This provides additional context about the at-risk renewal beyond the CRM deal record.
OE 16: Send an email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to yusuf.abdi@canopyhealth.io acknowledging his construction noise complaint and unresponsive coordinator experience, apologizing for the delay, and providing concrete next steps such as investigating the apartment situation, coordinating with building management for a noise remediation timeline, and exploring alternative apartment options if needed. The email should convey ownership of the issue and set a clear follow-up timeline.
OE 17: Send an email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to ines@pivotpointlabs.com acknowledging the Wacom Cintiq damage, apologizing for the lack of response from both her coordinator and account manager, and providing concrete next steps including initiating the damage claim process, clarifying responsibility between MoveOps and Swift Relocations, and setting expectations for resolution timeline.
OE 18: Send an email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to kevin.tran@vectralsystems.com acknowledging the delays and confusion around his stipend requests, providing honest status on both items: the monitor arm mount (recStip000000014, $129) remains in Pending status with no reviewer assigned and is being escalated to Finance, and the keyboard reimbursement has an active audit flag under investigation that cannot be dismissed or expedited. The email should NOT repeat any of Omar's fabricated claims, should avoid making promises about timelines that cannot be guaranteed, and should establish Julian as Kevin's single point of contact going forward given the sensitivity of the Vectral renewal.
OE 19: Send an email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io informing her that MoveOps is aware of Dr. Abdi's post-move issue with construction noise at his apartment, that Julian is personally overseeing the resolution, and providing a summary of the corrective steps being taken. This prevents relationship damage with Canopy Health's HR Director who was CC'd on the escalation.
OE 20: Send an email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to sam@pivotpointlabs.com informing him that MoveOps is aware of the equipment damage during Ines Kovac's relocation, apologizing for the communication gap, and outlining the damage claim process and expected resolution timeline. Sam Delgado is the CEO who handles relocation requests directly and is already asking Ines how the move went.
OE 21: Send an email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com addressing the Kevin Tran stipend situation with particular care given the active at-risk contract renewal (deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk). The email should acknowledge the service friction Kevin experienced, clarify that the stipend issues are being resolved through proper channels, and avoid making promises that cannot be kept -- especially given that the keyboard audit flag involves a legitimate investigation that cannot simply be dismissed.
OE 22: Create a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement) for Canopy Health documenting Dr. Abdi's post-move construction noise complaint, Blessing Okafor's unresponsiveness, the April 21-23 complaint timeline, Dr. Abdi's escalation to support, Patricia Langford being CC'd, and the corrective actions taken. Use engagement_type "NOTE" (or similar), with company_ids including "company_canopy".
OE 23: Create a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement) for PivotPoint Labs documenting Ines Kovac's damaged Wacom Cintiq drawing tablet, the Swift Relocations $125 electronics handling surcharge, Blessing Okafor's and Catalina Dubois's unresponsiveness, and the corrective actions taken. Use engagement_type "NOTE" (or similar), with company_ids including "company_pivot".
OE 24: Create a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement) for Vectral Systems documenting Kevin Tran's stipend routing problems, Omar Ibrahim's unauthorized and fabricated reassurances, the actual status of both stipend items (Monitor Arm Mount still Pending, keyboard audit flag is a fraud investigation not routine), and the sensitivity of this situation given the at-risk Q3 renewal. Use engagement_type "NOTE" (or similar), with company_ids including "company_vectral".
OE 25: Update the existing Linear issue (issue_compound_postmove_complaints_20260425) with investigation findings using linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_compound_postmove_complaints_20260425" and body summarizing findings across all three complaint threads (or similar), or linear_update_issue to update the description or state. The comment or update should cover: Abdi's construction noise and coordinator silence, Kovac's equipment damage and dual unresponsiveness, Tran's stipend routing failure and Omar's fabrication, the common pattern of Blessing Okafor's unresponsiveness across two accounts, CRM gaps identified and fixed, and client stakeholder communications sent.
OE 26: Post a message in Slack #customer-support using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C007" and payload correcting Omar Ibrahim's earlier claims about Kevin Tran's stipend situation. The message should clarify that the monitor arm mount stipend (recStip000000014) remains Pending with no reviewer assigned and cannot be expedited by support, and that the keyboard audit flag is part of an active fraud investigation (duplicate receipt hash) and is NOT a routine quality check that will drop off automatically. This prevents other team members from acting on Omar's misinformation.
OE 27: Send an email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com providing the initial read David requested. The email should summarize findings across all three complaint threads: (a) Dr. Abdi / Canopy -- construction noise complaint, Blessing Okafor unresponsive since April 21, escalation to support April 23, Patricia Langford aware; (b) Ines Kovac / PivotPoint -- Wacom Cintiq damaged during Swift Relocations move, Blessing and Catalina both unresponsive, Sam Delgado asking questions; (c) Kevin Tran / Vectral -- bounced between Zara, Mina, and Finance with no resolution, Omar fabricated claims about nudging reviewer queue and dismissing the audit flag which is actually a fraud investigation, at-risk renewal with Rachel Whitfield; (d) cross-cutting pattern: Blessing Okafor unresponsive on two separate accounts, CRM records showed no complaints, Omar made unauthorized representations. Include actions already taken: emails sent to affected employees and client stakeholders, CRM engagements created, Linear issue updated, Slack correction posted.

✗Run #6The agent used crm_search_companies and crm_get_contact to look up company metadata and contact details, but never called crm_list_engagements or crm_search_deals to verify existing engagement or deal records.

tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✗ 83%

✗Run #6No Linear tool calls of any kind appear in the entire trajectory. The agent never called linear_list_issues or any other Linear tool, meaning it did not discover the existing issue before taking action (or inaction) on Linear.
